task: Write a Python function sum_distinct(nums) returning the sum of the distinct values in the list, each value counted once no matter how many times it repeats. An empty list returns 0. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The provided function correctly sums distinct values in a list, but only one edge case (an empty list) is explicitly handled.
trace: The function creates a set from the input list, converting any duplicate values into unique items, then calculates the sum of all elements in the set. This guarantees that each number is only counted once, making it possible to add up the distinct values in the list. An empty list returns 0 because an empty set has no elements and the `sum()` function on an empty iterable returns 0. The edge case we handle here is when the input list is empty.
